title: Answering questions about me from Seamus 5/5 stoned

transcript.text: Alright, I have questions for SeamusFive.
Um, and I'm stoned while doing this.
Maybe I should smoke some weed.
What day would you love to relive?
Uh, the day I was born, for sure.
Um, what is your favorite kind of music?
Um, I like, like, upbeat, like, dance type of music.
I like Katy Perry, and Lady Gaga, and Kesha.
Yeah, stuff with the catchy beat.
Um, are you attracted by superpowers?
Uh, if you could have one, what would it be?
Um, I would be, uh, I'd be, uh, immortal.
With what power would you make money with it?
I'd learn, I'd want a superpower where I can read people's minds.
Um, who's been the most significant person to you other than your mom?
Uh, what movie do you watch again and again and not get bored of at all?
When I was a kid, I liked Herbie.
Um, and for some reason, my brother and I, we were a little bragged that we watched 9 to 5, like, 30 times or something.
But what movie do I really, really, really like and I could watch over and over?
Um, what's your most priced possession you would not share with anyone in my car?
Because, uh, like the transmission grind sometimes and suddenly, like, fluid can come out of the water cooler and I'm just, uh, paranoid about my car.
Um, why won't you cover the ghetto speaker behind your head?
Well, my stereo doesn't work, but it, like, just suddenly stopped working.
So I need to take it to the place and be like, yeah, it keeps coming out.
Do you know what the problem is?
Like, maybe, I don't know what happens, but, um, but it's a nice stereo system.
I should get it fixed one of these days when I have some extra money.
I think I could fix it for only, like, 30 bucks.
But I, uh, maybe I could put a mirror back there.
Um, what's one childhood memory that makes you smile?
Oh, just when I look back at my...
I don't have a specific memory that I can think of right now, but when I look back on, um...
I like living and hanging out in the house and stuff.
Yeah, but I can't think of a specific memory.
Um, have you ever been in a fistfight to usually win or talk your way out of it?
Um, in fifth grade, this kid pushed, was, like, insisting on keeping all the lights in the bathroom turned off.
So I turned it on, and he turned off, and then he pushed me.
Yeah, and then, when I was in college, there was, like, four or five, like, Hispanic gangster kids, like, started punching on my friend.
So I got off my bike.
I got off my bike, and I started punching them, and then they all started punching me, and my friend ran off.
And then I ran back to, like, my house.
They grabbed my bike, so I fought one of them for a few minutes.
But then, like, I kind of got out of breath, and I was like, I don't want to fight anymore.
We were basically taking turns punching each other, and then he charged me.
And I beat up his back, and then we fell down.
And then I jumped up, and I was like, I don't want to do...
I don't want to fight anymore.
Then the cops came, and they found my bike, like, one block down.
And then the cops, like, yeah.
Confiscated all the paraphernalia in the house.
Have you ever been picked on?
Have you ever been the bully?
I don't recall ever being a bully.
I've had kids, like, you know, like, say, like, snide remarks to you and stuff.
But I never got, like, jumped.
What do you think the school system should do about this problem?
I guess bullies are just kids who, like, don't know how to socialize,
And they just want to, like, um, get a rise out of other kids.
What do you think of school system?
I guess the little kids should be encouraged to talk about all of their problems to the teacher.
What's the worst lie you ever told your mom?
Can't ask, can't answer that one.
Ooh, that, like, when I get home after the day, my car's missing.
Or, like, I leave my backpack at a supermarket, and I walk off,
And then I lose my fucking computer and everything in it.
My backpack, my earphones, and Jesus, that'd suck.
And what is the most dangerous thing you have ever done?
Um, oh, I rode my bike from, um, um, La Paz down to Coroico.
And it's down this, uh, dirt road.
It's, like, on the edge of sheer cliffs with no guardrail.
And it's got all these snake turns.
And I was, like, bombing down it, like I was freaking on a bike race.
That was stupid, because if my rear wheel had it locked up,
And I had my back on the back frame, the back rack,
If it had, like, fallen down and, like, locked up the wheel like it does sometimes,
I would have been, like, forced to go straight and ride up the cliff.
And, like, a couple weeks before that, this, uh, Israeli girl did that.
She was on this rental, this, like, tour group, riding down it,
And she fell off and died.
And they saw, like, Israelis were talking about it.
And then I met some girl in, um, Argentina who, like, was with her.
But, yeah, that was, in retrospect, that was really dangerous.
Um, I wish that was stupid.
Should have, like, taken it easy down there.
Um, what is your most embarrassing moment as a kid?
Um, I remember one time, for fun, we were, like, loosening up the, uh,
Wheels on my friend's thing, bike.
And once he, like, found out it was me, like,
And he, like, in front of the host class, he was like,
Do you prefer the sunrise or sunset?
Uh, I would say the sunrise, because if I'm up when the sun rises,
Then I'm going to have a productive day.
I want to start getting up to the sunrise.
Number one, probably, um, Las Vegas,
Because I could see, like, all those Las Vegas shows.
Um, or maybe, I guess the number two would be...
No, no, number one would be New York,
Because I've never been there, and it's really big.
Number two, Las Vegas, I guess.
And then number three, uh, Hawaii on the beach.
Number four would be India, living like a king.
And number five would be touring, um, touring Egypt, I guess.
Okay, number one would be my kayak trip.
Number two would be to ski to the South Pole.
Number three would be, like, rowboat,
All the way around the world.
Oh, yeah, number four would be, I guess,
To hike to the North Pole.
Number two would be to ride my bike across Siberia.
What's one thing in which occurred in the past
That you would like to change?
Um, oh, wow, only ten minutes.
Um, oh, when I inherited my money and my dad died,
I spent all of it on, like, well, I got just,
I think $6,000 was, or something like that,
And then, um, yeah, I got, like,
The stereo system for this car.
I got, like, fixed up my bikes,
Because I was planning on living in my van.
I got a bunch of kayak stuff.
Yeah, but if I had just spent that and traveled,
I'm going to travel a long time.
What do you best like about yourself?
What do you think about the number of students
Um, um, I guess it should be about 30.
If God granted you three wishes,
Then what would they be and why?
For everybody else's wishes to be granted.
I wouldn't need the other two wishes.
And high school kids should take
Now that you're older and wiser?
The most valuable choice is assuming
You're going straight into the workforce
How to manage your life courses,
How to manage your life courses.
How to manage your life courses.
What kind of courses would that be?
I think kids already know English
And then number six would be
Where would you birth your survivors?
If a rooster lays an egg
I want to be with you forever.
I think that's a pretty cool compliment.
I cried when I found out
As bad as a 13 year old.
Talked to a lot of stuff
